MARKET_NAME: Food Safety Testing & Analytical Laboratory Services
CERTIFICATIONS: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 (A2LA Certificate 6041.01 -- Chemistry, Certificate 6041.02 -- Biological Testing)
KEY_CLIENTS: RGV produce growers, regulatory agencies (USA and Latin America)
INDUSTRIES_SERVED: Agriculture, Produce, Food Safety, Petroleum Geochemistry
COMPETITORS: Bureau Veritas, Eurofins Scientific, SGS SA
AVATAR: Quality Assurance Manager or Owner at a mid-size produce company in South Texas needing fast, local, accredited pesticide and micro testing
TOP_BLIND_SPOT_1: The only dual-accredited ISO 17025 lab in South Texas has a website with zero content
TOP_BLIND_SPOT_2: No service pages -- produce growers searching for pesticide testing find nothing
TOP_BLIND_SPOT_3: A2LA accreditation is invisible online -- the strongest trust signal in the industry is missing from the site
PITCH_ANGLE: The only ISO 17025 dual-accredited lab in South Texas -- and the website says nothing
STRATEGIC_FINDING: Empiria Labs holds dual A2LA accreditation that most labs in the region cannot match. The website presents as if the business does not exist.
DREAM_OUTCOME_1: Produce companies in the RGV find Empiria Labs on Google before shipping samples to Houston or San Antonio
DREAM_OUTCOME_2: A2LA dual accreditation is the first thing every visitor sees -- instant credibility
DREAM_OUTCOME_3: Sample submission form on the website eliminates phone tag and speeds turnaround
DREAM_OUTCOME_4: The site positions Empiria as THE food safety testing authority in South Texas
OFFER_NAME: The 90-Day Lab Visibility System for South Texas Testing Laboratories
GUARANTEE_LAYER_1: 30-day satisfaction guarantee -- any page redesigned free if not satisfied
GUARANTEE_LAYER_2: 1 qualified inbound inquiry within 90 days of launch or we keep working free
Service 4: Petroleum Geochemistry (TOC, Pyrolysis, Rock Eval, Biomarkers)
Description of their digital strengths: Major competitors have full-service websites with online sample submission, accreditation badges front and center, detailed service catalogs, and strong Google presence across multiple locations
Source 2 (Month Year): Grand View Research Analytical Laboratory Services (Feb 2026)
Source 3: USDA Produce Safety Alliance Annual Report 2025
Driver 1: FSMA Produce Safety Rule enforcement increasing demand for third-party testing
Driver 2: Nearshoring and cross-border produce trade growth in South Texas
Driver 3: Consumer demand for pesticide-free and organic certification
Driver 4: Retail and foodservice supply chain audit requirements tightening
Primary Deliverable: Lab Visibility Engine Website -- accreditation-forward, mobile-ready, sample submission integrated
Bonus Name: South Texas Lab Authority SEO Blueprint
Buyer description: QA Manager or Owner at a $1M-$50M produce operation in South Texas or Northern Mexico
Problem description: Cannot find a local accredited lab and currently ships samples to Houston, losing 3-5 days of turnaround
Fix #2: Build dedicated service pages for each testing category with technical detail
Fix #3: Add A2LA accreditation badges and downloadable certificates to every page
Fix #4: Implement an online sample submission form as the primary conversion tool
